title: grep
content:
grep word filename → matches any occurrence (even inside other words)
grep -w word filename → matches only exact whole word
grep -i word filename → case-insensitive match
grep -v word filename → shows lines that do not match
grep -n word filename → shows matching lines with line numbers
grep -c word filename → prints count of matching lines
grep -r word dir → searches recursively inside directory
grep -l word *.txt → shows only filenames that contain match
grep -L word *.txt → shows filenames that do not contain match
grep -E 'a|b' file → matches multiple patterns (OR condition)
grep -F 'text' file → matches exact string (no regex, faster)
grep -e a -e b file → multiple search patterns
grep -o word file → prints only the matched part
grep -A N word file → shows N lines after match
grep -B N word file → shows N lines before match
grep -C N word file → shows N lines before and after match
grep -q word file → no output, only exit status (use in scripts)
grep --color=auto word file → highlights matched text

title: misc
content:
grep -n word file | tail -1 | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -I{} tail -n +{} file | head -100
  • grep -n → get line numbers of matches
  • tail -1 → pick last match
  • cut -d: -f1 → extract line number
  • tail -n +{} → start printing from that line
  • head -100 → limit to next 100 lines
